description:
Originally part of fz-ul4.23.5.10 scope (\"Migrate REPL + test_runner onto ir_interp\"). The test_runner migration landed there; REPL migration deferred to here because it needs design work the .5.10 atom isn't sized for.

## Why a real REPL migration is non-trivial

The current REPL is a natural AST-level driver: each input either appends a fn definition to eval::Interp.globals or evaluates an expression against a persistent eval::Env (`repl_env = interp.globals.child()`). The test `binds_variable_across_inputs` (repl.rs:251) asserts that `x = 7` on one line and `x + 35` on the next produces `42` — fz's `=` is a pattern-match-bind that mutates the current frame, and the REPL relies on that frame persisting.

ir_interp has no native concept of a persistent cross-input env: each `run_fn` constructs its own Var → FzValue map indexed by IR Var ids, and Var ids are minted fresh per `ir_lower` pass.

## Plausible designs

A. Session as a single growing fn body. Each new input is appended as a fz statement; lower + re-run the whole accumulated fn each time. Variables persist naturally. Cost: previous prints re-fire each line.

B. Name-keyed session env. Translate REPL variable bindings to a synthetic outer scope (`__repl_scope`), keyed by name. Each input is wrapped as a fn that reads/writes that scope through accessor builtins.

C. Hybrid: keep the AST evaluator (eval::Interp) for the REPL since the REPL is inherently AST-level. Limit fz-ul4.23.5.10's framing to test_runner, the part that really benefits from IR-level parity.

Recommend evaluating C — eval::Interp's runtime entry points (load_program, call_named, eval) are load-bearing for macro expansion anyway, so it's not going away. The \"AST evaluator is compile-time only\" framing in fz-ul4.23.2's commit is partially aspirational.

## Acceptance

Whichever design lands:
- `fz repl`: type `1 + 1`, get `2`.
- `fz repl`: type `x = 7` then `x + 35`, get `42`.
- All five existing repl tests still pass (or the design ticket explicitly retires any of them with rationale).
title: Migrate REPL onto ir_interp (session-state design needed)
